# AI Bracelet Virtual Try On

## Overview
The Ultimate AI Bracelet Virtual Try-On
Employ AI-powered solutions to assist your customers with online purchases, ensuring perfect fit and great shopping satisfaction every time. Only One 2D Image Needed.

Create a compelling shopping flow with the hyper-realistic bracelet virtual try-on experiences. Our solution caters to the needs of jewelry brands of all sizes. Opt for 2D images for effortless yet high-quality virtual try-on experiences with minimal effort. This unique feature sets us apart in the world of e-commerce, making it easier than ever for customers to experience your products.

### Integration Guide
This guide walks you through:

*   **Endpoint:** `/s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/bracelet`
*   **Authentication:** All requests require an `Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`
*   **Workflow:**
    1.  **Prepare a wrist image:** Uploading an image or provide a valid image URL of your wrist
    1.  **Prepare a bracelet image:** Uploading an image or provide a valid image URL of a bracelet product
    1.  **Fire an AI task and Retrieve Task ID:** Capture the `task_id` from the response.
    1.  **Poll Status (`GET`):** Use the `task_id` to check the status of the task. Continue polling until `task_status` is `"success"` or `"error"`.

---

* API Playground

Interactively explore and test the API using our official playground:

**API Playground:**
[http://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-playground/ai-bracelet-virtual-try-on/](http://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-playground/ai-bracelet-virtual-try-on/)

---

* Authentication
- Include your API key in the request header using **Bearer Token**:
    ```
    Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
    ```
You can find your API Key at https://yce.makeupar.com/api-console/en/api-keys/.


* 1. Upload an Image

You may upload a file directly to the server or provide a valid image URL in the VTO task payload.

   * Upload Endpoint

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/bracelet
```

Alternatively, skip this step if you already have a public image URL.

You may upload a file directly to the URL provided in the response from the File API and then use the corresponding `src_file_id` returned by the File API to invoke the AI task later. Or provide a valid image URL in the VTO task payload as `src_file_url`. The `src_file_id` or `src_file_url` will serve as the virtual try-on target.

You must also provide another bracelet product image as a reference using `ref_file_ids` or `ref_file_urls` to be applied to your `src_file_id` or `src_file_url`.

The AI engine supports automatic background removal for your bracelet product image. However, you may provide an occlusion mask image file for either your hand (`srcmsk_file_id` or `srcmsk_file_url`) or the bracelet product (`refmsk_file_ids` or `refmsk_file_urls`) to fine-tune the segmentation.

---

* 2. Create a Bracelet VTO Task and Poll for Results

Once you have an image and a template ID, create a task. The API processes the request asynchronously. You must poll the task status until it reaches `success` or `error`.

   * Create Task Endpoint

```
POST /s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/bracelet
```

   * Polling Endpoint

```
GET /s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/bracelet/{task_id}
```

---

### File Specs & Errors

* AI Bracelet Virtual Try-On Specification

**Supported Bracelet View**
A bracelet image must be provided in a three-quarter front view (approximately 45 degrees).

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/bracelet_product_09_2cb9721d77_2f8d90ab9f.jpg)

**Supported Wrist View**
The back of the wrist should be fully visible with all five fingers clearly shown and without any occlusion.

![](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/watch_and_bracelet_user_01_09f16603cb_878dc89179.jpg)

**bracelet\_wearing\_location: float (−0.3 to 1.0)**
Indicates the position along the wrist:
−0.3 represents near the main wrist joint
1.0 represents far from the main wrist joint
Default value: null (use engine default)

![bracelet_wearing_location](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/bracelet_wearing_location_01ac0a048e.jpg)

**bracelet\_shadow\_intensity: float (0.0 to 1.0)**
Controls the strength of the shadow:
0.0 represents no shadow
1.0 represents maximum shadow
Default value: 0.15

**bracelet\_ambient\_light\_intensity: float (0.0 to 1.0)**
Defines the extent to which lighting references the target hand image:
0.0 ignores the hand image lighting
1.0 fully matches the hand image lighting and shadow rendering
Default value: 1.0

**Bracelet Anchor Points: array of 2 points in pixel coordinate (optional)**
Marks the inner edge of the bracelet where it contacts the wrist, specifying the left and right points.
If this parameter is not provided, the AI engine will automatically detect the anchor points.
![bracelet_anchor_point](https://bcw-media.s3.ap-northeast-1.amazonaws.com/strapi/assets/bracelet_anchor_point_c353245edc.jpg)

---

* Supported Formats & Dimensions

|AI Feature|Supported Dimensions|Supported File Size|Supported Formats|
|  ----  | ----  | ----  | ----  |
|AI Bracelet Virtual Try-On|long side <= 4096 |< 10MB|jpg/jpeg/png|

* Error Codes

|Error Code|Description|
|  ----  | ----  |
| RUNTIME_ERROR | An unexpected error occurred dubracelet runtime |
| PHOTO_DETECTION_FAIL | The user photo could not be processed correctly, for example no hand detected |
| OBJECT_DETECTION_FAIL | The object photo could not be processed correctly, for example no product detected |
| PHOTO_CHECK_INVALID | The pose or size of the user photo is invalid |
| INPUT_ERROR | The input file format is incorrect |
| INPUT_MAIN_IMAGE_EMPTY | A user image is required |


* Environment & Dependency

| Sample Code Language / Tool | Recommended Runtime Versions |
|---|---|
| cURL | - bash >= 3.2</br>   - curl >= 7.58 (modern TLS/HTTP support)</br>   - jq >= 1.6 (robust JSON parsing) |
| Node.js (JavaScript) | Node >= 18 (for global fetch) |
| JavaScript | - Chrome / Edge >= 80</br>   - Firefox >= 74</br>   - Safari >= 13.1 |
| PHP | PHP >= 7.4 (for modern TLS/compat), ext-curl (recommended) or allow_url_fopen=On + ext-openssl, ext-json |
| Python | Python >= 3.10 (for f-strings), requests >= 2.20.0 |
| Java | Java 11+ (for HttpClient), Jackson Databind >= 2.12.0 |

## Authentication

| Name | Type | Scheme / Location | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| BearerAuthenticationV2 | http | bearer | Use the standard 'Bearer authentication'. Put your 'API Key' in header: `Authorization:Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`. Notice that there is ' ' a space between 'Bearer' and the 'YOUR_API_KEY'. |

## Endpoints

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/file/2d-vto/bracelet`
**Create a new file.**

To upload a new file, you'll first need to use the File API. It will give you a URL – use that URL to upload your file. Once the upload is finished, you can use the file_id from the same response to start using our AI features.

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `FileV1.1`

This object represents a file.

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | Yes | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | Yes | Content MIME type for this file. Currently available values are listed in the enum. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of this file |
| `files.file_size` | `integer` | Yes | Content length for this file in bytes. Should not be larger than 10MB. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful retrieval of file upload information

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicFileResponse`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `files` | `array<object>` | No | — |
| `files.content_type` | `string` | No | Content MIME type for this file. |
| `files.file_name` | `string` | No | Name of this file |
| `files.file_id` | `string` | No | ID of this file. Other run task API will need this \`file_id\`. |
| `files.requests` | `array<object>` | No | Using upload \`url\`, \`headers\`, \`method\` below to upload file. After completion, the \`file_id\` is used to proceed with calling run task API. |
| `files.requests.headers` | `object` | No | Headers to include when uploading the file |
| `files.requests.url` | `string` | No | URL to upload this file |
| `files.requests.method` | `string` | No | HTTP method to upload this file |

**400** — Invalid request parameters

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
| `error_code` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `POST` `/s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/bracelet`
**Run an AI 2D Virtual Try On Bracelet task.**

This endpoint initiates the bracelet virtual try-on process. You must provide source file(s) and reference image(s) (via URL or File ID), along with specific parameters for alignment and shadowing. The task will be processed asynchronously, and you can check its status using the task_id returned in this response.

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Request body

**application/json** · required

**Schema:** `object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileUrls & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefmskFileUrls | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileUrl & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileIds & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefmskFileIds | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileUrls & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefmskFileUrls | object & BasicRunTaskV2SrcFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcmskFileId & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefFileIds & 2dVtoRunTaskV2RefmskFileIds & 2dVtoRunTaskV2SrcInfo & 2dVtoRunTaskV2ObjInfos & object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `src_file_url` | `string` | Yes | Url of the file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `srcmsk_file_url` | `string` | No | Url of the source mask file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `ref_file_urls` | `array<string>` | Yes | Url of the reference file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `refmsk_file_urls` | `array<string>` | No | Url of the reference mask file to run task. The url should be publicly accessible. |
| `ref_file_ids` | `array<string>` | Yes | ID of the reference file file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `refmsk_file_ids` | `array<string>` | No | ID of the reference mask file file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `src_file_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `srcmsk_file_id` | `string` | No | ID of the source mask file to run task. File ID from upload file API. |
| `source_info` | `object` | Yes | Contain source photo informations. |
| `source_info.name` | `string` | Yes | The source url or File ID. |
| `source_info.mask_name` | `string` | No | The source mask url or File ID. |
| `object_infos` | `array<object>` | No | — |
| `object_infos.parameter` | `object` | No | — |
| `object_infos.parameter.bracelet_need_remove_background` | `boolean` | No | Whether the bracelet image needs background removal. |
| `object_infos.parameter.bracelet_anchor_point` | `array<array<integer>>` | No | The anchor point of the bracelet image, marking the 2 farthest points along the inner edge of the bracelet <br>Type: 2x2 list of integers <br>Range: 2 (x, y) pixel coordinates <br>Default: null (use engine default) |
| `object_infos.parameter.bracelet_wearing_location` | `number` | No | Defines the placement of the bracelet on the wrist in the user photo. <br>Range: -0.3 ~ 1.0 (0 = closest to the wrist joint) <br>       (smaller number = placed close to the palm <br>       larger number = placed further away from the wrist) <br>Default: null (use engine default) |
| `object_infos.parameter.bracelet_shadow_intensity` | `number` | No | Shadow intensity applied to the bracelet (higher = darker shadow). |
| `object_infos.parameter.bracelet_ambient_light_intensity` | `number` | No | Ambient light adjustment intensity for the bracelet. |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful execution of the task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `BasicRunTaskResponseV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `data` | `object` | No | — |
| `data.task_id` | `string` | No | ID of this task. Task result is valid to query by this ID for 24 hours. |

**400** — Failed execution of task

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `RunError`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | Error message |
| `error_code` | `string — InvalidParameters \| CreditInsufficiency \| InvalidStyleGroup \| InvalidStyle \| BadRequest` | No | Error code:   * InvalidParameters - Invalid request parameters   * CreditInsufficiency - Insufficient unit to run   * BadRequest - Unexpected request parameter   * InvalidStyleGroup - Invalid style group id   * InvalidStyle - Invalid style id |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**429** — Too many requests

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

### `GET` `/s2s/v2.0/task/2d-vto/bracelet/{task_id}`
**Check the status of a AI 2D Virtual Try On Bracelet task.**

**Tags:** V1.0

**Authentication:** BearerAuthenticationV2
#### Parameters

| Name | In | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `task_id` | path | `string` | Yes | ID of task to check |

#### Responses

**200** — Successful check of the task status

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `TaskStatusResponseBodySingleUrlResultsV2`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `url` | `string` | No | URL to download this result. Valid for 2 hours |

**400** — Invalid task ID

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**401** — Invalid or missing API key

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |

**500** — Task execution timeout

Content types: `application/json`

**Schema:** `object`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `status` | `integer` | No | Response status |
| `error` | `string` | No | — |
